title: Cavity Filling: Cusp Build-Up
description: Step-by-step composite restoration of a large cavity with cusp replacement, by Dr. Javier Tapia Guadix.

decisionPoints.stepDescription: The cavity has been prepared and isolated. Etchant is about to be applied.
Matrix and wedge are in place. The doctor is about to begin composite placement.
Multiple composite layers have been placed. The doctor is sculpting occlusal anatomy.

instrument.name: Rubber Dam Clamp
instrument.description: Provides moisture isolation critical for composite bonding. The Tofflemire matrix band maintains proximal wall contour.

referenceContent.technique: Large MO/MOD cavities with cusp involvement require incremental composite layering to minimize polymerization shrinkage and restore occlusal anatomy.

instrument.name: Phosphoric Acid Etchant
instrument.description: 37% phosphoric acid (blue gel) applied to enamel for 15-30 seconds, dentin for 10-15 seconds. Creates micro-porosities for mechanical bonding.

referenceContent.technique: Total-etch technique: etch enamel margins first, then dentin. Over-etching dentin collapses collagen fibrils and weakens the hybrid layer.

instrument.name: LED Curing Light
instrument.description: Polymerizes the adhesive bonding agent. Minimum 800 mW/cm² output. Hold 1-2mm from the surface for 10-20 seconds.

referenceContent.technique: Apply adhesive in thin layer, gently air-dry to evaporate solvent, then light cure. A well-polymerized adhesive layer is the foundation of the entire restoration.

instrument.name: Tofflemire Matrix Band
instrument.description: Circumferential metal band held by a retainer. Creates a temporary proximal wall to contain composite during placement.

referenceContent.technique: Wedge firmly at the gingival margin to ensure tight proximal contact and prevent composite flash. The matrix must be contoured to recreate natural tooth anatomy.

instrument.name: Composite Instrument
instrument.description: Used to place and pack composite resin into the cavity in increments of 2mm or less.

referenceContent.technique: Incremental layering technique: place 2mm layers obliquely to reduce C-factor stress. Each increment is light-cured before placing the next. Build from the deepest floor toward the occlusal surface.

instrument.name: Dental Explorer / Carver
instrument.description: Fine-tipped instruments used to sculpt cusps, ridges, and fissure patterns into the final composite layer before full polymerization.

referenceContent.technique: Recreate natural cusp anatomy: marginal ridges, triangular ridges, and central fossa. Final layer can be shaped before curing for better anatomical detail than post-cure finishing.

instrument.name: Finishing Bur / Explorer
instrument.description: Fine-grit diamond burs or carbide finishing burs remove excess and refine occlusal contacts.

referenceContent.technique: Check occlusion with articulating paper. Remove premature contacts. Polish with graded discs or polishing paste for smooth, plaque-resistant surface.
